commands.review_summary:
The branch updates `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` so no-active-items `rawResponseItem/completed` notifications keep the completion-idle watchdog armed, and it also carries the queued-terminal-notification guards from the companion queue-delay PR.

PR surface: Source +27. Total +27 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: no. not at high confidence. The PR body has credible pre-fix stall logs and source inspection supports the disarmed-watch path, but this review did not reproduce the failing current-main run or inspect after-fix live output.
commands.review_followup: Human review should decide the timeout-boundary tradeoff, require contributor-provided after-fix proof, and resolve the stacked companion PR before merge.

target.title: fix(codex): arm completion idle watch for stalled binary after rawResponseItem/completed
target.body:
## Summary

- Arm the 60s completion idle watch when `rawResponseItem/completed` arrives with all tracked items completed and no active requests, so a stalled codex binary is detected in 60s instead of 30 minutes
- Prevent `rawResponseItem/completed` in this state from disarming an existing completion idle watch

## Root cause

When the codex binary relays `rawResponseItem/completed` from the OpenAI Responses API and all `item/completed` notifications have already arrived (`activeTurnItemIds` empty, `activeAppServerTurnRequests === 0`), the binary should deliver `turn/completed` imminently. If the Responses API fails to send `response.completed` to the binary, `turn/completed` never arrives and the turn stalls.

Previously, only `rawResponseItem/completed` notifications that qualified as `postToolRawAssistantCompletionNeedsTerminalGuard` (type=message, role=assistant, has text preview, post-tool-handoff) would arm the completion idle watch. Any other type of `rawResponseItem/completed` would actively **disarm** the watch via the catch-all disarm block (lines 2508-2526), leaving only the 30-minute `turnTerminalIdleTimeout` to catch the stall.

## Evidence

Gateway stall detector from 2026-05-27 (post-#86948 fix, on current main including #87022):

```
00:03:17 stalled session: lastProgress=rawResponseItem/completed lastProgressAge=137s recovery=none
00:04:17 stalled session: lastProgress=rawResponseItem/completed lastProgressAge=197s recovery=none
00:05:17 stalled session: lastProgress=rawResponseItem/completed lastProgressAge=257s recovery=none
00:06:17 stalled session: lastProgress=rawResponseItem/completed lastProgressAge=317s recovery=none
00:07:17 stalled session: lastProgress=rawResponseItem/completed lastProgressAge=377s recovery=checking
```

The binary sent its last `rawResponseItem/completed` and went silent for 377s+ with no `turn/completed`. The completion idle watch was disarmed, so only the 30-minute terminal timeout would have eventually caught this.

## Changes

**`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts`:**

1. Add `rawResponseItemCompletedWithNoActiveItems` — true when `rawResponseItem/completed` arrives on the current turn with `activeTurnItemIds.size === 0`, `activeAppServerTurnRequests === 0`, and `postToolRawAssistantCompletionNeedsTerminalGuard` is false (to avoid double-arming)
2. Arm `turnCompletionIdleWatch` when this condition is true (60s timeout for `turn/completed` to arrive)
3. Exclude this condition from the catch-all disarm block so the watch stays armed

## Expected behavior after fix

When the binary stalls after `rawResponseItem/completed`, the 60s completion idle timeout fires instead of waiting 30 minutes. The turn is aborted and the client is retired (via #87022's cleanup). Users see a ~60s stall instead of a ~30 minute hang.

Refs #87071, #86948, #87022, PR #87070.
cc @steipete @Peetiegonzalez
target.author: marvinthebored
